shared_properties: The emitter uses the same hard-edge palette discipline and art deco hardware language as other puzzle props.
Every frame preserves transparent-background compositing so beams and masks can be layered independently in Bevy.
Gold hardware and amber light stay inside the project palette and never blur into the background architecture.
differences: This asset is the primary danger-source visual and therefore carries the highest luminance contrast in the package.
The atlas frames communicate pulse timing rather than positional movement; rail motion is runtime-driven.
The nozzle geometry is more directional than the mirror or prism so players can parse beam origin immediately.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load moving_light_emitter.png during Loading, build a 3x2 atlas for pulse states, and register author-time beam anchor offsets used by Rapier2D raycast systems.
runtime_notes: The sprite only represents the emitter core and authored beam origin; the actual beam path is drawn by the runtime light system.
Support fixed, moving, and focused light variants by swapping beam logic while keeping one consistent core silhouette.
Keep the beam anchor centered on the brightest core pixel so raycasts visually originate from the same point the player perceives.

context.usage: Represents the visible core for fixed, moving, and focused lights that define danger zones and drive shadow generation.

readability_notes: The core remains the brightest non-UI element before the runtime beam is drawn.
A clear nozzle direction indicates beam forward orientation even when the core is idle.
Pulsing frames increase intensity without changing the anchor point.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 192x128 px for six 64x64 frames with no frame gutter.
The beam origin sits on the same pixel coordinate across all six frames.
At 50% preview scale the emitter direction remains readable without the runtime beam attached.
